text:
Market today: Chip stocks are bouncing and inflation fears are cooling, which is genuinely good for your tech-heavy book. The real driver is sentiment shifting back toward growth names - exactly what you need given your concentration.

Your holdings: AVGO at -14.26% is your biggest problem - it's your largest position, on margin, and still flagged TRIM. That combo means a continued slide hits you harder than anyone else. PLTR at -11% is flagged SELL - you're underwater and holding a name the scanner is telling you to exit. PTNM is -15.25% with only a thin cushion above its $9.50 stop - tiny spec, but watch it. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited history; margin amplifies any sudden drop fast.

Watch: 1) Whether today's chip rebound has legs - that directly saves or sinks AVGO. 2) PLTR holding above $85 stop; if it cracks, margin math gets ugly quick.

Bottom line: Your two biggest risks are both on the SELL/TRIM list - act on AVGO and PLTR before the market decides for you.
